gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: TWAP manipulation via oldestObservation fallback and fixed 10% slippage allows attacker to drain protocol value during swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn
candidate.claimed_root_cause: getTorusQuoteForTitanX uses a TWAP with an oldestObservation fallback that collapses the window on low-cardinality pools; _swapTitanXForTorus sets amountOutMinimum at only 90% of the (potentially manipulated) TWAP quote; swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn is permissionless, letting the attacker time the protocol swap after manipulating the TWAP.
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker pre-sells TORUS at fair value to depress pool price, waits for TWAP to reflect manipulated price, triggers protocol swap at deflated amountOutMinimum, then buys TORUS back cheaply — capturing the spread at the protocol's expense.

live_preconditions.name: active titanX burn interval with non-zero amountAllocated
live_preconditions.observed_value: lastTitanXBurnIntervalNumber=53087; totalTitanXBurn=390047518149437255354831991687 (non-zero pool); interval amountAllocated not directly captured but pool is active

live_preconditions.name: TitanX/TORUS pool observation cardinality (oldestObservation)
live_preconditions.observed_value: not captured in live_context; pool address derived from torusToken.titanXTorusPool()

live_preconditions.name: TitanX/TORUS pool liquidity depth (to assess manipulation cost)

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: TWAP oracle read with oldestObservation fallback: if oldestObservation < 900, secondsAgo is reduced to oldestObservation, collapsing the TWAP window toward spot and making it trivially manipulable.

amountOutMinimum is set to expectedTorusAmount * (100 - titanXToTorusSlippage) / 100 = 90% of the TWAP quote. If TWAP is manipulated below fair value, the floor is proportionally lower and the swap succeeds with far fewer TORUS received.
External, permissionless (no caller restriction beyond nonReentrant and burnTitanXIntervalUpdate). Attacker controls the timing of the protocol swap relative to their TWAP manipulation window.
titanXToTorusSlippage = 10 (10%). A TWAP depressed by more than 10% passes the amountOutMinimum check while delivering materially fewer TORUS than fair value.

INITIAL_TITAN_X_FOR_LIQ = 200_000_000_000e18 and INITIAL_LP_MINT = 20_000e18 set the initial pool ratio; TORUS side is thin relative to TitanX, increasing susceptibility to price impact on the TORUS side.

attacker_path.actor: any unprivileged EOA or contract with sufficient TitanX and TORUS capital (or flash-loan access)
attacker_inputs: Size of TORUS → TitanX swap to depress pool price
Duration to hold manipulated price (up to secondsAgo = min(oldestObservation, 900))
call_sequence: 1. Attacker acquires TORUS (spot purchase or flash-loan from pool).
2. Attacker executes large TORUS → TitanX swap on the TitanX/TORUS Uniswap V3 pool, depressing the TORUS spot price.
3. Attacker waits secondsAgo seconds (up to 900s) for the TWAP accumulator to reflect the depressed price.
5. Inside _swapTitanXForTorus, getTorusQuoteForTitanX reads the manipulated TWAP → returns deflated expectedTorusAmount.
6. amountOutMinimum = deflated_quote * 90 / 100 — a very low floor.
7. Uniswap router executes the swap at the still-depressed spot price; protocol receives far fewer TORUS than fair value. Swap passes amountOutMinimum check.
8. Attacker reverses position: buys TORUS back at the depressed price (protocol swap pushes price back up), capturing the spread.
state_after: Protocol received fewer TORUS than fair value; fewer TORUS burned
Attacker net positive: sold TORUS at fair price, bought back at depressed price

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: TORUS tokens (protocol burn value) and TitanX (attacker's price-manipulation spread)
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: Price spread between fair TORUS value and depressed TORUS price at time of protocol swap; attacker buys back TORUS cheaper than they sold it.
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol burns fewer TORUS per TitanX interval than fair market value; the deficit in burned TORUS represents permanent protocol value drain (reduced deflationary pressure).
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: The attacker is entitled to the 1.5% INCENTIVE_FEE for calling the function (by design), but not to the additional spread captured by manipulating the TWAP oracle that sets the swap's minimum output floor.

setup: Fork Ethereum mainnet at a recent block where liquidityAdded = true (confirmed in live context).
Resolve pool address: call torusToken.titanXTorusPool() on 0xb47f575807fc5466285e1277ef8acfbb5c6686e8.
Read pool observation cardinality and oldestObservation via OracleLibrary.getOldestObservationSecondsAgo(poolAddress).
Ensure titanXIntervalsBurn[lastTitanXBurnIntervalNumber].amountAllocated > 0 and amountBurned == 0; if not, call distributeTitanXForBurning to seed a new interval.
Deal attacker a large TORUS balance (or use vm.prank + flash-loan simulation).
transaction_sequence: 1. Attacker executes large TORUS → TitanX exactInputSingle on the pool to depress spot price.
6. Attacker executes TitanX → TORUS swap to buy back TORUS at depressed price.
expected_assertions: assertLt(torusReceived, fairQuote * 90 / 100, 'protocol got less than 90% of fair value');
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If torusReceived >= fairQuote * 90 / 100 or attackerProfit <= 0, the pool has sufficient liquidity depth or observation cardinality to make the attack unprofitable under current live conditions; downgrade to REJECT or low severity informational.
